County

Kenton, KY

FIPS: 21117 · 32,765 Medicare beneficiaries · Geographic Variation 2023

OverviewBeneficiary ProfileMedicare SpendingUtilizationHealth OutcomesPreventionHealth Risk BehaviorsSocial NeedsRelated FacilitiesAll DataMethodology
Bottom quartile Middle Top quartile Percentile badges (e.g. p50) rank this county against the 3,198 counties with data. Lower-is-better metrics (costs, readmissions, disease prevalence, etc.) use an inverted color ramp.

Overview

Key headline metrics for Medicare beneficiaries living in Kenton, KY. Source data from the CMS Medicare Fee-for-Service Geographic Variation Public Use File (2023) and CDC PLACES/SDOH.

Total Medicare Spending per Capita p79
$12,951
national median $11,472 · standardized (payment adjustments removed)
Actual Spending per Capita
$12,705
includes local geographic adjustments
Average HCC Risk Score p91
1.09
1.00 = nationally average. Higher = sicker panel.
Average Age
72.0
30-Day Readmission Rate p82
19.2%
national median 16.8%
MA Participation
59.5%
share enrolled in Medicare Advantage
Dual Eligible p29
11.3%
also enrolled in Medicaid — low-income marker

Medicare Spending per Capita

Share of standardized per-capita Medicare spending going to each service setting. Bars are this county; the red line marks the national county median. Standardized dollars remove local payment adjustments so counties can be compared fairly.

Utilization

How often Medicare beneficiaries use each service. Rates are per 1,000 beneficiaries; the "users" column shows the share of beneficiaries who used the service at least once.

30-Day Readmission Rate p82
19.2%
national median 16.8%
Share of hospital discharges followed by an unplanned readmission within 30 days.

602.6
national median 600.7

30.0%
national median 30.0%

Health Outcomes

CDC PLACES — modeled prevalence of health outcomes among county adults. Values are age-adjusted or crude prevalence estimates with 95% CIs.

All teeth lost among adults aged >=65 years p2
7.1%
95% CI: 5.7% – 8.8% · national median 15.2%
Crude prevalence

Prevention

CDC PLACES — modeled prevalence of prevention among county adults. Values are age-adjusted or crude prevalence estimates with 95% CIs.

Mammography use among women aged 50-74 years p71
76.3%
95% CI: 68.5% – 82.7% · national median 74.0%
Age-adjusted prevalence
Colorectal cancer screening among adults aged 45–75 years p90
68.0%
95% CI: 63.3% – 72.4% · national median 60.8%
Crude prevalence
Visited dentist or dental clinic in the past year among adults p78
64.8%
95% CI: 61.1% – 68.3% · national median 59.5%
Crude prevalence

Health Risk Behaviors

CDC PLACES — modeled prevalence of health risk behaviors among county adults. Values are age-adjusted or crude prevalence estimates with 95% CIs.

Short sleep duration among adults p58
38.0%
95% CI: 32.0% – 44.1% · national median 36.9%
Age-adjusted prevalence

Health-Related Social Needs

Community-level social and economic factors that drive health outcomes. Combines CDC PLACES health-related social-need measures (modeled from BRFSS) with CDC SDOH structural measures (from the American Community Survey).

Economic Stability

Below 150% Federal Poverty Level p27
18.7%
national median 23.2% · MOE ±1.2
Share of county residents living below 150% of the federal poverty threshold.
Unemployment Rate p34
4.2%
national median 4.9% · MOE ±0.5

8.5%
national median 10.8% · MOE ±0.6

Share of households without a broadband internet subscription — a telehealth access barrier.

Related Facilities & Organizations

Medicare-participating hospitals, SNFs, and ACOs that serve this county.

  • hospital ST ELIZABETH EDGEWOOD 4-star overall rating
  • hospital SUN BEHAVIORAL HEALTH
  • snf THE PAVILION AT KENTON 1-star, 82 beds
  • snf Rosedale Green 3-star, 176 beds
  • snf Madonna Manor 1-star, 60 beds
  • snf ST ELIZABETH EDGEWOOD SNF 5-star, 30 beds
  • snf Village Care Center 4-star, 100 beds
  • snf Woodcrest Nursing and Rehabilitation Center 4-star, 127 beds
  • snf VILLASPRING OF ERLANGER 3-star, 140 beds
  • snf Emerald Trace 2-star, 54 beds

Geographic Variation White Beneficiaries (%) 93.6% 89.1% p77 BENE_RACE_WHT_PCT

Methodology

Primary source: Medicare Geographic Variation by County (dataset id geo-var-county, vintage 2023, downloaded 2026-04-13, 33,639 rows in source).

Additional data sources: CDC PLACES County-Level Data (2026), CDC SDOH Measures for County (2017-2021).

Percentile badges compare this county against all US counties that appear in the relevant dataset. "Lower is better" metrics (spending, readmission rates, chronic-disease prevalence, SDOH adversity) use an inverted color ramp — low percentile = green (good), high percentile = red (bad).

Medicare Geographic Variation shows standardized per-capita spending, utilization, and demographic characteristics for Medicare fee-for-service beneficiaries at the county level. Standardized payments remove geographic payment adjustments so counties can be compared fairly. AHRQ Prevention Quality Indicators (PQIs) measure the rate of hospitalization for ambulatory-care-sensitive conditions (ACSCs).

Full methodology →

MIT License. Free. No ads. No login.
GitHub · Report an error
